name: fancy chain mail
shortDescription: Fancy chain mail polishes ordinary links into something court guards can wear without looking like stable mercenaries. The rings are still meant to catch blows, but they do so with a little more social ambition.
flavorText: “A polished link protects no better, but it does flatter the payroll.”

name: Redvale general armor
shortDescription: Redvale general armor is severe, red-marked, and built for command in places where speeches must compete with drums. Its plates carry the tone of a campaign map: direct, expensive, and never as clean after use.
flavorText: “Redvale did not dress its generals to hide them.”

name: fancy ring mail with gem
shortDescription: Fancy ring mail with a gem is a compromise between battlefield caution and courtly vanity, which is often where expensive armor lives. The gem marks the owner as someone who expects both danger and compliments.
flavorText: “If the mail must be heavy, the jewel may as well carry some of the conversation.”

name: fabled solid gold breastplate
shortDescription: A fabled solid gold breastplate is absurd, magnificent, and heavy with every bad idea wealth can polish. Few warriors would choose it for a long campaign, but many rulers would choose it for the portrait afterward.
flavorText: “Gold makes poor armor and excellent propaganda.”

name: orange crystal platemail
shortDescription: Orange crystal platemail glows through its facets like banked sunset sealed over a warrior’s chest. It is rare, spectacular, and probably more magical than any quartermaster wants to insure.
flavorText: “Some armor reflects the blow; crystal armor makes the blow reconsider its purpose.”

name: ironclan chain mail
shortDescription: Ironclan chain mail is rugged, close-linked, and stubbornly practical, made by smiths who trust repetition more than ornament. It feels like something hammered under mountains and sold only after surviving an argument.
flavorText: “Ironclan mail is not pretty, unless endurance is your idea of beauty.”

name: ironclan leather armor
shortDescription: Ironclan leather armor is reinforced for hard travel, with dark straps, heavy stitching, and metal touches where softer gear usually gives up. It is leather armor for people who expect stone, weather, and rivals to test the receipt.
flavorText: “The Ironclan even makes leather sound like a hammer.”

name: ironclan platemail
shortDescription: Ironclan platemail is all broad plates, squat strength, and little patience for decoration that cannot take a blow. It carries the mountain-forge attitude plainly: if it bends, it was not finished.
flavorText: “Ironclan plate is less worn than endured.”

name: ironclan ringmail
shortDescription: Ironclan ringmail uses large, heavy rings joined with uncompromising craft, better suited to strong shoulders than delicate taste. It is excellent gear for warriors who believe comfort should prove itself after victory.
flavorText: “Every Ironclan ring looks like it was closed during an argument.”

name: platemail with gold
shortDescription: Platemail with gold trim turns practical defense into a public statement of rank. The steel does the surviving, but the gold ensures everyone understands whose survival was considered worth decorating.
flavorText: “Gold-trimmed plate says the wearer has enemies and sponsors.”

name: ring mail with leather
shortDescription: Ring mail with leather backs its metal rings with tough hide, giving the armor a grounded, road-ready feel. It is less elegant than court mail, but better suited to mud, saddles, and the sort of fight that starts without music.
flavorText: “Leather remembers the road; rings remember the blade.”

name: fancy ring mail with leather
shortDescription: Fancy ring mail with leather dresses a practical design in cleaner cuts, finer straps, and metalwork meant to be noticed. It is armor for someone who might ride patrol in the morning and dine under chandeliers by night.
flavorText: “A good strap holds armor closed; a fancy one holds reputation together.”

name: salamander platemail
shortDescription: Salamander platemail is heat-themed armor with ember colors, scale shapes, and the suggestion that ordinary fire should mind its manners. Whether forged from salamander lore or simply inspired by it, the result looks ready for burning roads.
flavorText: “Salamander plate is what a forge wears when it dreams of war.”

name: scale mail
shortDescription: Scale mail is a proven armor of overlapping plates, flexible enough for movement and tough enough to turn many clean cuts into ugly complaints. It has served soldiers, guards, and caravan captains who prefer old answers that still work.
flavorText: “Scales win by sharing the blow.”

name: scale mail with expensive gems
shortDescription: Scale mail with expensive gems is protection dressed for a victory parade, every jewel daring the battlefield to notice the owner. It is wildly impractical in the best way: costly, memorable, and likely guarded even when nobody wears it.
flavorText: “Gemmed armor protects two things: the body and the story told about it.”

name: heavy platemail
shortDescription: Heavy platemail is unapologetic armor, built for the kind of fighting where mobility has already lost the debate. It is punishing to wear, expensive to move, and deeply comforting once the arrows start behaving personally.
flavorText: “Heavy plate is faith measured in pounds.”

name: fabled spiked ironclan breastplate
shortDescription: A fabled spiked Ironclan breastplate turns mountain-forged protection into an open threat. The spikes are not subtle, the plates are not light, and the whole piece appears to have been designed by someone tired of polite warfare.
flavorText: “Ironclan spikes are not decoration; they are punctuation.”

name: splint mail
shortDescription: Splint mail binds long metal strips over a sturdy backing, making armor that looks simple until a weapon fails to find a soft answer. It is a soldier’s compromise: cheaper than full plate, braver than cloth, and hard enough for bad roads.
flavorText: “Splint mail is what thrift wears when it still expects trouble.”

name: strong leather armor
shortDescription: Strong leather armor is thick, treated, and confidently stitched, made for scouts and caravan guards who need protection without clanking like tax collectors. It smells of oil, hide, and many careful evenings by the workbench.
flavorText: “Good leather armor bends before it breaks, which is more than most plans do.”

name: studded leather armor
shortDescription: Studded leather armor reinforces tough hide with metal points, rivets, or plates placed where trouble most often lands. It is common for sellswords because it offers a little steel without demanding a blacksmith follow them everywhere.
flavorText: “Studs are the leatherworker’s way of saying no twice.”

name: mire brute hide
shortDescription: Mire brute hide is thick, swamp-dark, and stubbornly resistant to both blades and good cleaning. It is prized by armorers who can stand the smell long enough to imagine what it becomes after the tannery has suffered.
flavorText: “Mire brute hide arrives ugly and leaves useful.”

name: mire brute leather armor
shortDescription: Mire brute leather armor turns swamp-monster hide into heavy protective gear with a dark, scarred finish. It feels primitive beside polished plate, but few wearers complain once a blade skids off the old brute’s skin.
flavorText: “Some armor is forged. Some is hunted, cured, and persuaded to keep being stubborn.”

name: fabled platemail of Unraveling
shortDescription: The fabled platemail of Unraveling is whispered to fray bindings, loosen old wards, and make hostile enchantments forget their own knots. It looks almost too still on a stand, like it is waiting for magic to make the first mistake.
flavorText: “The armor does not break spells; it teaches them how to fall apart.”

name: iron boots
shortDescription: Iron boots are heavy, blunt, and designed for people who expect the ground to be less dangerous than whatever stands on it. They protect the feet well, announce every step badly, and make stealth a philosophical problem.
flavorText: “Iron boots are excellent for marching and terrible for sneaking out.”

name: shield of Pearlgate
shortDescription: The shield of Pearlgate bears coastal shaping, pale ornament, and the proud defensiveness of a city that learned to face both raiders and storms. It is not merely a shield, but a portable harbor wall for one determined arm.
flavorText: “Pearlgate shields were painted bright so the sea could find its defenders.”
